title: Widget and Label Fonts
text: Widget and Label Fonts A font in Uniface has a logical name and a physical name. The physical font names are system-specific. The logical font names are mapped to their physical names in the initialization file for the GUI. You can specify the font to use for both the data displayed in a widget and the widget label. All widgets have the following font properties: Widget Font —font used in the widget itself. For example, the Widget Font for a drop-down list determines the font that Uniface uses for the items in the list itself. To ensure that the insertion character displays correctly, make sure that the size of the widget font is less than or equal to the size of the system font (Font 0). If the widget font is larger than the available space in the widget, the widget can behave unpredictably at runtime. For example, Microsoft Windows hides the cursor and truncates the text at the bottom. To correct this, choose a smaller font or enlarge the size of the widget. Alternatively, define a bigger font for screen Font 0. This results in all forms appearing larger on the screen. Label Font —font used in the label associated with the widget. For this definition to work, you must have associated the label with the widget, or have loaded the label and widget via Load Fields. It is recommended that you specify the label font for a widget in its properties, rather than in the .ini file. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
